authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Sector 56 and Subsector 561 Administrative and Support Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. industry taxonomy used to define the administrative-support and business-services operating boundary and prevent generic compliance treatment.

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative and Support Services: NAICS 561
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public industry profile confirming administrative and support service operating contexts used to shape records, workforce, and service-queue assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry terminology and operating context validation

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official records-management requirements source for capture, metadata, retention, disposition, access, and auditability expectations in administrative records systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records capture, evidence retention, disposition, and traceability

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government, GAO-25-107721
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public internal-control framework used for control design, operation, documentation, monitoring, deficiencies, and remediation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control mapping, deficiency handling, monitoring, and remediation

authoritative_sources.title: Evaluation of Corporate Compliance Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Criminal Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official compliance-program guidance supporting risk assessment, policies, training, reporting, investigations, third-party management, data access, and remediation effectiveness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Policy, training, reporting, escalation, and remediation workflow

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Privacy Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary public privacy-risk framework used to structure privacy and personal-data handling caveats for administrative service records.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative control catalog for access control, audit/accountability, configuration, privacy, and evidence controls used by administrative service systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access control, audit logging, and system evidence checks

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 37301:2021 Compliance management systems - Requirements with guidance for use
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized international compliance-management standard for establishing, evaluating, maintaining, and improving compliance systems and obligation handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Compliance management system structure and continuous improvement

authoritative_sources.title: SOC 2 - SOC for Service Organizations: Trust Services Criteria
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized service-organization assurance reference for controls relevant to security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy in client service operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client audit evidence and service-organization control readiness

workflow_stages.name: Intake and administrative-service classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, service family, client/SOW scope, jurisdiction, business unit, owner matrix, and system-of-record context before mapping controls.

workflow_stages.name: Obligation, policy, and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map client contract, tenant policy, records, privacy/access, service quality, and compliance-program expectations to controls and evidence owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence assembly and traceability
workflow_stages.description: Collect and validate client master records, SOWs, tickets, queue logs, SOPs, training attestations, retention schedules, access reviews, redaction logs, exception records, and remediation artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict, exception, and remediation escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate conflicts among public baseline, tenant policy, client contract, operational practice, privacy/access requirements, and records obligations when authority shifts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Review, audit, and attestation preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence checklists, control summaries, remediation status, and assumption logs for internal review or client audit while refusing final certification or legal signoff.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Client master record, contract, SOW, service catalog, work order, and SLA commitments
Ticket/case queue, service logs, queue metrics, fulfillment status, and escalation notes
Policy library, SOPs, version history, training and policy acknowledgments
Records inventory, retention schedule, document repository, redaction log, chain-of-custody log, legal-hold flags, and disposal approvals
Access roster, IAM extracts, access-review approvals, terminated-user checks, and audit logs
Exception register, issue log, remediation tracker, CAPA record, and management response
Client audit request, service-organization control evidence, prior findings, and attestation-owner communications
systems_of_record: Client relationship management or client master system
Contract lifecycle management and SOW repository
Ticketing, workflow, or case management system
Document management or records management system
Policy management and training attestation system
SLA reporting or business intelligence system
Email/correspondence archive where tenant policy names it as authoritative

allowed: Classify administrative compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Prepare evidence-backed obligation-control matrices, evidence checklists, control-gap summaries, and remediation routing notes.
Determine whether visible controls and evidence are satisfied, missing, stale, conflicting, or require escalation.
escalate: Tenant service model, jurisdiction, client contract, policy hierarchy, record location, access owner, or system-of-record facts are missing.
Public baseline, tenant policy, client contract, and operational practice conflict in ways that materially change outcome.
Legal, privacy, security, HR/payroll, finance, procurement, audit, or executive approval authority is required.
refuse: Request asks for legal advice, formal certification, regulator negotiation, or final client-facing signoff.
Request asks the lane to approve a policy exception, contract amendment, financial commitment, HR/payroll decision, or security incident determination.
Request would close controls without required evidence, owner traceability, or current system-of-record confirmation.

domain_failure_modes: Treating the industry name as cosmetic and issuing a generic compliance checklist that ignores client work orders, service queues, records repositories, SLA evidence, and access/control systems.
Closing a compliance review when SOW, SOP, training, ticket, retention, access, or remediation evidence is missing or stale.
Resolving conflicts between client contracts, tenant policies, public frameworks, and operational practice without authorized precedence.
Confusing administrative evidence handling with legal advice, formal audit/certification, HR/payroll authority, security incident response, or executive approval.

Using one administrative-service template across materially different service families such as office administration, call centers, collections, travel support, facilities support, and security services.
Ignoring records retention, legal hold, redaction, privacy/access, or chain-of-custody constraints when preparing client audit evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence due missing facts or system status

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Which administrative service family controls the artifact set
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: NAICS 561 contains materially different service families; runtime must retrieve service-family, jurisdiction, client contract, and adjacent-lane context before case-specific output.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client contract versus tenant policy precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Precedence must be supplied by tenant legal/compliance or contract owner when a client SOW and tenant policy materially disagree.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether SOC-style evidence is for internal readiness or formal attestation
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane can prepare evidence checklists and control summaries but cannot issue an audit opinion, certification, or formal service-organization attestation.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains: ambiguous points are converted into mandatory tenant fact requests or escalation triggers.
Source set spans named-industry terminology, records workflow, compliance program operation, control evidence, privacy/access, and service-organization assurance boundaries.
The lane stays boundary-safe and does not provide legal advice, final signoff, audit opinion, certification, policy override, HR/payroll authority, or security incident authority.
